source.multipart_basis: Decomposition derived from CanSat-class 350 ml beverage-can form factor: aeroshell halves + base/top plate + internal electronics deck + parachute canister + recovery bay + battery bracket mirror the canonical CanSat primary structure. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the primary aluminum structural chassis of a CanSat payload as a CanSat-class primary structure benchmark. The chassis houses the flight computer, GPS receiver, sensors, radio, battery, and parachute recovery system and must survive launch on a sounding rocket and subsequent parachute descent from approximately 1 km AGL.

Geometry. The CanSat primary structure is a thin-walled cylindrical enclosure with outer diameter 66 mm and total length 115 mm, sized to fit inside the standard 350 ml aluminum beverage-can envelope used as the reference form factor. Wall thickness is 1.2 mm. The cylinder closes with a circular base plate (thickness 2.0 mm) and a removable top plate (thickness 2.0 mm) held in place with four M3 stainless-steel socket-head cap screws. Internal bulkheads carry the electronics stack (two PCBs, 58 mm x 58 mm) and a 3.7 V 1500 mAh LiPo cell. The parachute attachment point is a single central eyelet on the top plate tapped for an M4 bolt with an engineered shear zone.

Material. The primary structure is machined from 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa). PCBs are modeled as 1.6 mm FR-4 with lumped component masses for non-structural analyses. Analyses are performed at 20 C.

Load cases. (LC1) Launch axial acceleration of 20 g (196.1 m/s^2) applied along the cylinder axis while the CanSat is clamped at its base by the carrier deployer. (LC2) Launch lateral vibration qualification: sine sweep 5-100 Hz at 2 g input applied laterally on the chassis. (LC3) Parachute deployment shock: 30 g 11 ms half-sine applied through the top-plate attachment point after the main chute opens at 300 m AGL. (LC4) Ground-impact hard landing: 40 g 5 ms half-sine applied through the base plate.

Pass/fail criteria. (R1) Maximum von Mises stress in the aluminum chassis must not exceed 184 MPa (yield / 1.5) for every load case. (R2) Maximum lateral displacement at mid-span must not exceed 1.5 mm (clearance to internal electronics). (R3) Total CanSat payload mass must not exceed 350 g as required by the CanSat-class rulebook. (R4) The first natural frequency of the chassis with populated electronics must be at least 120 Hz to avoid coupling with the rocket airframe fundamental. (R5) Under the 30 g deployment shock, the M4 parachute-attachment boss must transfer load without permanent deformation (plastic strain < 0.002).

Solver and analysis expectations. The primary verification is linear static structural FEA for LC1, LC3, and LC4. Modal (vibration) analysis is required to compute the natural frequency in R4. Harmonic response for LC2 may be approximated as a sine-sweep check on modal participation. Thermal and fluid analyses are not required. Radiation analysis is not applicable.

Deliverables. STEP (AP242) + Parasolid of the full primary structure, a mass budget table, and a report with the four stress extrema, the first three natural frequencies, and the deployment-shock stress concentration at the eyelet.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This CanSat primary structure is delivered as a multi-part assembly matching the typical CanSat practice: (A) cylindrical aeroshell body tube (66 mm OD x 115 mm, 1.2 mm wall), (B) base plate with deployer-clamp interface (2.0 mm), (C) removable top plate with central M4 parachute eyelet and engineered shear zone (2.0 mm), (D) internal electronics-deck bulkhead(s) locating the two 58 x 58 mm PCBs, (E) parachute canister / recovery bay above the top bulkhead, (F) battery bracket holding the 3.7 V 1500 mAh LiPo cell, and (G) the four M3 SHCS top-plate fasteners plus internal standoff fasteners. FEM verification runs on the assembled model only; per-part stress is reported. All inherited load magnitudes (LC1-LC4), the 6061-T6 property set, and R1-R5 pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria (R_asm1 connection DCR, R_asm2 eyelet shear-zone load path integrity) are added.
short_prompt:
Design the primary aluminum structural chassis of a CanSat payload as a CanSat-class primary structure benchmark. The chassis houses the flight computer, GPS receiver, sensors, radio, battery, and parachute recovery system and must survive launch on a sounding rocket and subsequent parachute descent from approximately 1 km AGL.

The CanSat primary structure is a thin-walled cylindrical enclosure with outer diameter 66 mm and total length 115 mm, sized to fit inside the standard 350 ml aluminum beverage-can envelope. Wall thickness is 1.2 mm. The cylinder closes with a circular base plate (thickness 2.0 mm) and a removable top plate (thickness 2.0 mm) held in place with four M3 stainless-steel socket-head cap screws. Internal bulkheads carry the electronics stack (two PCBs, 58 mm x 58 mm) and a 3.7 V 1500 mAh LiPo cell.

Material: 6061-T6 aluminum (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield strength 276 MPa, ultimate tensile strength 310 MPa).

Load cases: LC1 launch axial acceleration of 20 g (196.1 m/s^2) along the cylinder axis; LC2 launch lateral vibration sine sweep 5-100 Hz at 2 g; LC3 parachute deployment shock 30 g 11 ms half-sine; LC4 ground-impact hard landing 40 g 5 ms half-sine.

prompt.title: CanSat-class primary structure benchmark (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Bolted Al 6061-T6 CanSat primary structure (66 mm OD x 115 mm, <=350 g total): cylindrical aeroshell body tube, base plate, removable top plate with M4 parachute eyelet, internal electronics deck, parachute canister, and battery bracket, tied by four M3 top-plate SHCS and internal standoff fasteners. Must pass 20 g axial, 2 g lateral sine 5-100 Hz, 30 g 11 ms parachute shock, 40 g 5 ms ground impact, and modal >= 120 Hz at the assembled level.

geometric_constraints.form_factor: CanSat 350 ml reference (66 mm OD x 115 mm length)

boundary_conditions.deployer_clamp: base clamped in carrier deployer during launch

objective.primary: pass CanSat-class rulebook + survive launch and landing loads

parts.name: aeroshell body tube
parts.role: primary thin-walled cylindrical pressure shell (aerodynamic + structural)

parts.name: base plate
parts.role: closes base of body tube; interfaces with carrier deployer clamp for LC1

parts.name: top plate (removable, with parachute eyelet)
parts.role: closes top of body tube; central M4 parachute eyelet with engineered shear zone for LC3 shock

parts.name: electronics-deck bulkhead
parts.role: internal bulkhead locating the 58 x 58 mm PCB stack

parts.name: parachute canister / recovery bay
parts.role: cylindrical bay above top bulkhead holding folded parachute and recovery electronics

parts.name: battery bracket
parts.role: holds 3.7 V 1500 mAh LiPo cell against launch / landing g-loads

parts.name: fastener set (top-plate M3 SHCS + internal standoffs)
parts.role: 4x M3 SHCS securing top plate to body tube plus M2.5 standoff fasteners for internal bulkheads

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: cylinder axis = +Z, base at Z=0, top plate at Z=115

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: avoid coupling with rocket airframe fundamental (typ 60-90 Hz)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across top-plate M3 fasteners, internal bulkhead standoffs, and base-plate weld / bolt interfaces <= 1.0 under governing shock case (LC3 or LC4).

Parachute eyelet load path integrity on assembled model: engineered shear zone must yield before the four M3 top-plate fasteners strip their threads under LC3 (fastener reserve margin >= 0.2 at peak eyelet load).

interfaces.mediated_by: circumferential lap joint (adhesive + press fit or TIG weld)
interfaces.load_path: base plate (B) reacts axial launch load (LC1) into aeroshell tube (A) along circumferential joint.

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M3 SHCS through top-plate flange into tapped holes in body tube lip
interfaces.load_path: top plate (C) bolted to body tube (A); LC3 parachute shock runs from eyelet through top plate into fastener circle and then shear into body tube.

interfaces.mediated_by: internal ledge / standoffs bolted to body tube inner wall
interfaces.load_path: electronics-deck bulkhead (D) hangs inside body tube (A); PCB inertia under LC1/LC4 reacts into tube through standoffs.
parachute canister (E) bolted to underside of top plate (C); parachute deployment reaction enters top plate through canister flange.

interfaces.mediated_by: M2.5 SHCS plus slot in bulkhead
interfaces.load_path: battery bracket (F) bolted to electronics-deck bulkhead (D); LiPo inertia under LC1/LC4 reacts through bracket into bulkhead and then into body tube.

fem_check_policy.rationale: CanSat mass cap and modal floor are defined on the fully populated, assembled can: per-part stress (eyelet, body tube, base plate) only becomes meaningful once the fastener ring and internal bulkheads close the load path.

notes.why_multi_part: A CanSat is physically a stack of plates, bulkheads, and a tube closed by a removable top plate; internal electronics access and parachute packing require it to be disassemblable, not monolithic.
notes.speculation_caveats: Number of internal bulkheads, canister height, and battery-bracket detail are programmatic speculation within CanSat-class practice.
